operations.name: Add Attachment to List Item

file_name.description: Name to assign to the attachment file in SharePoint.

content_base64.description: Optional base64-encoded bytes to upload. Use for binary content when not passing file_content.

FileNameAsPath.description: File name as path object with DecodedUrl
FileNameAsPath.title: File Name As Path

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

operations.name: Add Role Assignment to List Item
operations.description: Tool to add a role assignment to a list item. Use when granting specific permissions to a user or group after breaking inheritance if needed. This action is externally visible and permanently alters item permissions; obtain explicit human approval for the target item and `role_definition_id` before executing.

item_id.description: ID of the list item to which to add the role assignment.

list_title.description: Title of the SharePoint list containing the target item. Must exactly match the list's display name.

principal_id.description: ID of the user or group to grant permissions.

role_definition_id.description: ID of the permission level (role definition) to assign.

item_id.description: ID of the list item that was updated.
item_id.title: Item Id

message.description: Success message indicating the role assignment was added.
message.title: Message

list_title.description: Title of the list where the permission was granted.
list_title.title: List Title

principal_id.description: ID of the user or group granted permission.
principal_id.title: Principal Id

role_definition_id.description: ID of the permission level assigned.
role_definition_id.title: Role Definition Id

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

operations.name: Add Role Assignment to SharePoint List
operations.description: Tool to add a role assignment to a SharePoint list. Requires the list to have broken role inheritance first via SHARE_POINT_BREAK_ROLE_INHERITANCE_ON_LIST; inheriting lists will reject unique role assignments. Use when granting permissions to a user or group on a specific list.

list_title.description: The title of the SharePoint list to grant permissions on.

principal_id.description: The ID of the user or group principal to which the role will be assigned. Must be a site-collection-registered ID; use the Id returned by SHARE_POINT_ENSURE_USER to avoid invalid-principal errors.

role_definition_id.description: The ID of the role definition to assign (e.g., 1073741827 for Contribute).

success.description: Indicates if the role assignment was added successfully.
success.title: Success

status_code.description: HTTP status code returned by the SharePoint API.
status_code.title: Status Code

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

operations.name: Break Role Inheritance on List Item
operations.description: Tool to break permission inheritance on a list item. Call this before adding new role assignments; adding assignments prior leaves the item still inheriting parent permissions, causing unexpected access behavior. Use when you need to uniquely set permissions on an item after copying or clearing parent assignments.

item_id.description: ID of the list item whose permissions inheritance will be broken

list_title.description: Title of the list containing the item Must be the exact display name as shown in SharePoint UI, not the URL segment or internal name.

clear_subscopes.description: Whether to clear unique permissions on any child scopes of the item

copy_role_assignments.description: Whether to copy existing parent role assignments to the item before breaking inheritance

message.description: Result message indicating success of breaking inheritance
message.title: Message

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

operations.name: Break Role Inheritance on List
operations.description: Breaks permission inheritance on a SharePoint list, allowing you to set unique permissions. When you break inheritance, you can choose to: - Copy parent permissions as a starting point (copy_role_assignments=true) - Start fresh with no inherited permissions (copy_role_assignments=false) - Clear unique permissions on child items to re-inherit from this list (clear_subscopes=true) Use this when you need to manage list-level permissions independently from the site. Must be called before SHARE_POINT_ADD_ROLE_ASSIGNMENT_TO_LIST, which fails on lists still inheriting from the parent site.

list_title.description: The title of the SharePoint list to break permission inheritance on.

clear_subscopes.description: Whether to reset unique permissions on child items (folders, documents) to inherit from this list. Set true to remove all item-level custom permissions and force inheritance from the list. Set false to preserve existing item-level unique permissions.

copy_role_assignments.description: Whether to copy parent permissions when breaking inheritance. Set true to start with a copy of the parent site's permissions (recommended for most cases). Set false to start with empty permissions where only the current user has access.

message.description: Success message indicating the permission inheritance was broken successfully.
message.title: Message

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

operations.name: Check In SharePoint File

comment.description: Check-in comment to describe this version of the file. Defaults to empty string if not provided.

checkintype.description: Check-in type: 0=MinorCheckIn (draft/minor version), 1=MajorCheckIn (published/major version), 2=OverwriteCheckIn (overwrite current version without creating new version). Defaults to 1 (Major version).

server_relative_path.description: Server-relative URL of the file to check in, including library and folders. E.g., '/Shared Documents/example.docx'.

status.description: HTTP status code returned by SharePoint for the check-in operation. 200 or 204 indicates success.
status.title: Status

message.description: Success message confirming the file has been checked in.

server_relative_path.description: Server-relative path of the checked-in file.
server_relative_path.title: Server Relative Path

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

operations.name: Create Drive Item Sharing Link
operations.description: Tool to create a sharing link for a drive item in SharePoint or OneDrive. Use when you need to generate a shareable link with specific permissions (view/edit/embed) and scope (anonymous/organization/users). The link can optionally include password protection and expiration date.

type.description: The type of sharing link to create. 'view' creates read-only access, 'edit' creates read-write access, 'embed' creates embeddable link (OneDrive Personal only).

drive_id.description: The unique identifier of the drive containing the item.

password.description: The password for the sharing link. Optional and OneDrive Personal only.

driveItem_id.description: The unique identifier of the drive item to create a link for.

expirationDateTime.description: The expiration time of the sharing link in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ). If not specified, the link does not expire unless a default expiration policy is enforced.

retainInheritedPermissions.description: If true (default), any existing inherited permissions are retained on the shared item. If false, all existing permissions are removed when sharing for the first time.

id.description: The unique identifier of the permission
id.title: Id

type.description: The type of the link (view, edit, or embed)
type.title: Type

scope.description: The scope of the link (anonymous, organization, or users)
scope.title: Scope

webUrl.description: A URL that opens the item in the browser
webUrl.title: Web Url

webHtml.description: For embed links, the HTML code for an iframe element to embed the item
webHtml.title: Web Html

application.description: Identity information for an application or user.
application.title: IdentityInfo

preventsDownload.description: If true, the user can only view the item on the web and cannot download contents
preventsDownload.title: Prevents Download

roles.description: The type of permission (e.g., read, write)
roles.title: Roles

shareId.description: A unique token for accessing this shared item via the shares API
shareId.title: Share Id

hasPassword.description: Indicates whether the link has a password set
hasPassword.title: Has Password

expirationDateTime.description: The expiration time of the permission in ISO 8601 format
expirationDateTime.title: Expiration Date Time

operations.name: Create List Item in Folder
operations.description: Tool to create a list item in a specific folder within a SharePoint list. IMPORTANT: This action works ONLY with SharePoint lists (not document libraries). The folder_url can be either a server-relative URL (e.g., '/Lists/MyList') or an absolute URL (e.g., 'https://site.sharepoint.com/sites/sitename/Lists/MyList'). To create files in document libraries, use a different action like upload_file.

FieldName.title: Field Name

FieldValue.title: Field Value

folder_url.description: URL of the target folder within the list where the item will be created. Accepts both server-relative URLs (e.g., '/Lists/MyList') and absolute URLs (e.g., 'https://site.sharepoint.com/sites/sitename/Lists/MyList'). For the list root folder, use '/Lists/<ListName>' or the full absolute URL. For a subfolder within the list, append the subfolder path. You can obtain this URL from the list's RootFolder.ServerRelativeUrl property. This parameter works with SharePoint lists only, NOT document libraries.

operations.name: Delete SharePoint Folder
operations.description: Deletes a folder from a SharePoint document library. This action permanently removes the specified folder and moves it to the site's recycle bin. Use this when you need to remove folders that are no longer needed. The operation is idempotent - attempting to delete a non-existent folder will succeed without error. Requires the server-relative path of the folder (e.g., '/Shared Documents/FolderName').

etag.description: ETag for conditional deletion. Use '*' (default) to delete regardless of version. Provide a specific ETag value to delete only if the folder hasn't been modified since that version.

server_relative_url.description: The server-relative path of the folder to delete. This is the full path from the site root, including the document library and any parent folders. Format: '/DocumentLibrary/ParentFolder/FolderName'. Examples: '/Shared Documents/Reports', '/Documents/2024/Q1'.

message.description: Success message confirming the folder was deleted
message.title: Message

server_relative_url.description: The server-relative path of the folder that was deleted, matching the input parameter
server_relative_url.title: Server Relative Url

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

operations.name: Ensure SharePoint User
operations.description: Ensures a user exists in a SharePoint site by their login name. If the user already exists, returns their info; if not, adds them to the site — this is a write operation with a provisioning side effect, not a read-only presence check. Use when you need to add a user or get their user ID for permissions. The login name must be in SharePoint claims format (e.g., 'i:0#.f|membership|user@domain.com'). This action only registers the user in the site collection and does not grant any permissions. To assign list-level access, pass the returned Id field as principal_id to SHARE_POINT_ADD_ROLE_ASSIGNMENT_TO_LIST.

login_name.description: Login name of the user in SharePoint claims format (e.g., 'i:0#.f|membership|user@domain.com'). This is typically obtained from user lookup actions like list_site_users or get_current_user. Throws an exception if login_name does not map to a real directory user; in batch operations, handle each user individually to prevent one failure from halting the batch.

operations.name: Get SharePoint List Changes
operations.description: Tool to retrieve changes from SharePoint list change log. Use when processing webhook notifications to get actual changes that occurred. Set boolean flags in query (Add, Item, Update, etc.) to filter change types. Store the ChangeToken from the last change and use as ChangeTokenStart in subsequent calls to track only new changes.

Tool to fetch SharePoint file metadata (exists/permissions/URLs/UniqueId) given a server-relative path, using the more robust ServerRelativePath API for special characters. Use after SHARE_POINT_SEARCH_QUERY returns a Path and you need to confirm the canonical working path or retrieve file properties without downloading bytes.

operations.name: Get SharePoint Folder by Server Relative URL
operations.description: Tool to retrieve a folder by its server-relative URL. Use when you need to fetch folder metadata by path before further operations. Returns only the immediate folder; to traverse nested structures, iterate `Folders.results` and call this tool recursively for each subfolder.

expand.description: OData $expand query option. Comma-separated list of related entities to expand, such as 'Files,Folders,ListItemAllFields'. Expanding Files or Folders can produce large responses; access nested data via `Files.results` and `Folders.results` arrays.

select.description: OData $select query option. Comma-separated list of fields to return. E.g., 'Name,ServerRelativeUrl,ItemCount'.

server_relative_url.description: Server-relative URL of the folder to retrieve, including library and any parent folders. Can be provided as a plain path (e.g., '/Shared Documents/MyFolder') or as a pre-encoded URL (e.g., '/Shared%20Documents/MyFolder'). Both formats are accepted and handled correctly. Path must exactly match the real SharePoint library name (e.g., '/Shared Documents' not '/Documents'); extra or missing slashes cause resolution failures. Prefer reusing the `server_relative_url` returned by this tool for downstream calls rather than constructing paths manually.

operations.name: Get SharePoint List Items
operations.description: Tool to retrieve items from a SharePoint list. Use when you need to fetch list entries with optional OData parameters.

top.description: Maximum number of items to return.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of navigation properties to expand for lookup fields.

filter.description: OData filter expression to limit results (e.g., Title eq 'Test').

select.description: Comma-separated list of internal field names to return. Use '*' to retrieve all available fields, which is recommended when you don't know the exact field names. SharePoint uses internal field names, not display names - spaces are hex-encoded as '_x0020_' (e.g., 'File_x0020_Size' for 'File Size'). Common system fields: Id, Title, Created, Modified, AuthorId, EditorId, GUID, FileLeafRef (file name), FileDirRef (folder path), FileRef (server-relative URL). Field availability varies by list type: document libraries include file-specific fields like File_x0020_Size, while custom lists have their own schema. To discover available fields for a specific list, either: (1) use select='*' first to see all fields returned in items, or (2) use SHARE_POINT_GET_LIST_BY_TITLE with expand='Fields' to get field metadata including InternalName.

orderby.description: OData orderby expression to sort results (e.g., Created desc).

list_title.description: Exact Title property value of the SharePoint list to query. The Title property value varies by site configuration and localization - for example, the default document library's Title may be 'Documents', 'Shared Documents', or a localized name. To find the exact Title property value, use SHARE_POINT_LIST_ALL_LISTS which returns all lists with their Title properties.

items.description: Array of list item objects returned from the SharePoint list.
items.title: Items

items.description:
Represents a SharePoint list item with standard system fields.

Additional custom fields defined in the list schema will be present but are not
typed here as they vary by list configuration.
items.title: SharePointListItem

uri.title: Uri

operations.name: Get User Effective Permissions on Web
operations.description: Get a user's effective permissions on the current SharePoint site (Web). This action retrieves the combined permissions a user has on the site, taking into account direct permissions, group memberships, and permission inheritance. Use this when you need to verify what permissions a user or group has before performing operations. The response contains permission masks as 64-bit integers split into high and low 32-bit values. A value of "0" for both means no permissions. Returns site-level permissions only; list-level or item-level grants and restrictions are not reflected.

login_name.description: Claims-based login name of the user or group whose permissions to retrieve. Provide the unencoded login; the action will URL-encode it automatically. Use list_site_users or get_current_user to obtain valid login names.

low.description: Low 32 bits of the 64-bit permission mask as a string integer. Represents permissions at bit positions 0-31. Common permissions like ViewListItems, AddListItems, EditListItems are in the low bits.
low.title: Low

high.description: High 32 bits of the 64-bit permission mask as a string integer. Represents permissions at bit positions 32-63.
high.title: High

operations.name: Log SharePoint Event
operations.description: Log custom usage analytics events in SharePoint for tracking user activities. Records usage events (views, edits, custom actions) to SharePoint's analytics system. Use this after performing actions to track usage patterns. The event will be logged to SharePoint's usage analytics database for reporting and analytics purposes. Common use cases: - Track document views or edits - Record custom user interactions - Log application-specific events for analytics Note: Obtain site/web GUIDs from GET /api/site or GET /api/web endpoints. Use consistent item_id, scope_id, and site GUIDs across related events for the same entity to ensure correct analytics grouping and correlation in reports.

scope_id.description: GUID defining the scope/context of the event. Typically the web ID or site collection ID where the event occurred.

event_type_id.description: ID of the usage event type. Valid values are typically 1-4 for different SharePoint event types (e.g., 1=View, 2=Edit, 3=Custom). Use values 1-4 for standard tracking events.

operations.name: Rename SharePoint Folder
operations.description: Renames a SharePoint folder by updating its list item metadata. To use this action: 1. First call GET_FOLDER_BY_SERVER_RELATIVE_URL with expand=ListItemAllFields to get the folder's metadata_type 2. Extract the '__metadata.type' value from the response's ListItemAllFields (e.g., 'SP.Data.Shared_x0020_DocumentsItem') 3. Call this action with the folder path, metadata_type, and new name The rename operation updates both the folder's display name (Title) and actual name (FileLeafRef). Note: This only changes the folder name, not its location in the hierarchy.

new_name.description: New folder name (leaf name only, not full path). This will be used for both the display name (Title) and the actual folder name (FileLeafRef). Do not include path separators (/).

match_etag.description: ETag value for concurrency control. Use '*' (default) to overwrite regardless of the current version. To enforce version checking, obtain the ETag from GET_FOLDER_BY_SERVER_RELATIVE_URL with expand=ListItemAllFields, then use the '__metadata.etag' value.

__metadata.type.description: OData entity type of the folder's list item. This is required by SharePoint's REST API. To obtain this value, first call GET_FOLDER_BY_SERVER_RELATIVE_URL with expand=ListItemAllFields, then extract the '__metadata.type' field from ListItemAllFields (typically 'SP.Data.Shared_x0020_DocumentsItem' for the 'Shared Documents' library, but varies by library name).

server_relative_folder_url.description: Server-relative path of the folder to rename, including library and any parent folders. Must start with '/' and include the document library name. Example: '/Shared Documents/OldFolder' or '/Shared Documents/ParentFolder/OldFolder'.

operations.name: Search SharePoint Site
operations.description: Search SharePoint content using Keyword Query Language (KQL). Returns documents, list items, folders, and other content matching your query. Supports filtering by properties (file type, author, date), pagination, and custom property selection. Results are nested under PrimaryQueryResult→RelevantResults→Table→Rows→Cells as Key/Value pairs requiring explicit extraction. Results are security-trimmed: inaccessible content never appears even if it exists. Use contentclass (e.g., STS_Site, STS_Web, STS_ListItem_DocumentLibrary) in querytext to isolate specific item types.

rowlimit.description: Maximum number of results to return per request. Default is determined by SharePoint (typically 50). Maximum is 500.

startrow.description: Zero-based index of the first result to return. Use for pagination (e.g., startrow=0 for page 1, startrow=10 for page 2 with rowlimit=10). Compare TotalRows vs RowCount in the response to determine if additional pages exist; increment startrow by rowlimit until all results are retrieved.

querytext.description: The search query using Keyword Query Language (KQL) syntax. Examples: 'project report', 'IsDocument:1', 'FileType:docx', 'Author:"John Doe"'. Broad queries (e.g., '.xlsx' alone) return noisy large result sets; narrow with site paths, Author, date ranges, or other KQL properties. Filter by contentclass to isolate specific item types.

selectproperties.description: Comma-separated list of managed property names to return in results. If not specified, SharePoint returns a default set of properties. Common properties: Title, Author, Path, Size, LastModifiedTime, FileType. Include SiteId and WebId alongside Path to disambiguate items sharing the same title across different locations.

refinementfilters.description: Refinement filters to narrow search results. Format: 'PropertyName:value' or use FQL syntax for complex filters. Example: 'FileType:equals("docx")'.

search_results.description: SharePoint search results with structured data including elapsed time, primary query results, properties, and metadata.
